summary: Ran backend pytest regression and frontend Playwright E2E against public preview URL for the CAOS shell/pipeline. Core session list/select, chat orchestration, receipt/WCW updates, and readable UI error surfacing all worked. No APIs were mocked in app code; one temporary Playwright route interception was used only to verify frontend error handling.

ui_bugs.issue: Receipt labels and values render without spacing (e.g., 'Reduction ratio0%'), reducing readability.

issues: Metric label/value typography spacing is cramped and reads as concatenated text.

action_items: Add visual spacing/separation between receipt metric labels and values in the right context column.
critical_code_review_comments: Shell user-email input updates state on every keystroke and retriggers bootstrap session loading via effect dependencies; consider submit/blur or debounce to avoid unnecessary API churn.
Chat route returns raw exception text in HTTP 500 detail; consider sanitizing server errors before exposing to clients.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed profile/session/message/memory records in preview DB during API and UI tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL validated. Backend pytest now includes /api/caos/chat persistence+receipt+wcw assertions in addition to session/message endpoints. Frontend E2E validated new-thread, send, left-rail selection, search filter, context receipt update, and readable error banner behavior.
rca of the issue: Receipt readability issue is caused by context metric content rendering inline without adequate spacing/styling between label text and numeric value in the metric card layout.
